summary: Eval cases cover the init-file guard, `/z-eval-help` with and without a topic, disambiguation, the skill resume loop, optional post-answer navigation, and README-grounded answers that mirror live project signals with proper citations.

cases.expected_output: Answer grounded in Configuration with citations and values from the host project files.
assertions: The agent loads zoto-help-evals once `help_context` includes `selected_section` matching the Configuration README heading.
The final assistant message contains at least one fenced code-reference block whose path suffix is `plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md` with numeric `start:end` line anchors.
The answer states at least one fact clearly tied to a file the workflow read (for instance `evalsDir`, manifest target cardinality, or a latest run id) rather than only generic plugin wording.
No askQuestion calls originate from zoto-help-evals during the skill run.
Every quoted answer carries a `<startLine>:<endLine>:plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md` citation tied to the README source.
Code-reference fences open with ```` ```<startLine>:<endLine>:plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md ```` so citations remain machine-checkable against the README.

cases.expected_output: Help addresses troubleshooting intent using README anchors while respecting skill input rules.
assertions: `help_context.user_question` records the free-form sentence instead of a numeric menu id.
zoto-help-evals returns a tailored reply without emitting askQuestion from inside the skill.
The reply still includes at least one `start:end:plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md` citation block for any quoted README material.
Every quoted answer carries a `<startLine>:<endLine>:plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md` citation tied to the README source.
Code-reference fences open with ```` ```<startLine>:<endLine>:plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md ```` so citations remain machine-checkable against the README.
follow_ups: My last static run failed pytest collection — which README section should I read and what should I check in this repo?

cases.scenario: direct topic without extra section menu
cases.expected_output: The host agent invokes zoto-help-evals with help_context anchored on the Quick start section and returns operator-facing prose that weaves in values read from this workspace plus README citations.
assertions: after `/z-eval-help Quick start`, the tailored prose names at least one project-specific datum drawn from `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml`, `manifest.yml`, `package.json` scripts, or `evals/_runs/` when that artefact is present in the workspace
the answer cites `plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md` using `start:end:plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md` for any quoted README excerpt
the command did not present the numbered full-section menu solely because a single `## Quick start` header uniquely matched the topic
observable behaviour shows zoto-help-evals ran without introducing its own askQuestion calls

cases.expected_output: User picks which LLM-related README section to anchor; answer follows that anchor with citations.
assertions: After `/z-eval-help LLM`, askQuestion lists only the competing `##` headers whose titles match the topic token before loading zoto-help-evals.
After the follow-up names a specific header, zoto-help-evals runs with `help_context.selected_section` set to that exact title.

The composed answer cites the chosen section via `start:end:plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md` references and obeys the read-before-write guidance from the skill for project signals tied to that anchor.
Every quoted answer carries a `<startLine>:<endLine>:plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md` citation tied to the README source.
Code-reference fences open with ```` ```<startLine>:<endLine>:plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md ```` so citations remain machine-checkable against the README.

follow_ups: I mean compare two runs side by side
cases.expected_output: When zoto-help-evals returns needs_user_input, the command issues askQuestion to capture the missing detail, then re-invokes the skill with an updated help_context until a final narrative answer is returned.
assertions: when the skill first returns needs_user_input, the command runs askQuestion before calling zoto-help-evals again with completed fields in help_context
after `/z-eval-help Overview` and the free-form follow-up turn, the final assistant text still honours the citation contract for `plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md`
the free-form follow-up text is captured on help_context as `user_question` per the documented skill contract

cases.expected_output: Command bridges a needs_user_input result into a completed help_context and a second skill call.
assertions: If zoto-help-evals first returns `needs_user_input`, the command runs askQuestion before the next skill invocation.
The subsequent zoto-help-evals call includes `help_context` fields that satisfy the skill’s requested enum or section candidates.
At no point does zoto-help-evals itself call askQuestion.
Every quoted answer carries a `<startLine>:<endLine>:plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md` citation tied to the README source.
Code-reference fences open with ```` ```<startLine>:<endLine>:plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md ```` so citations remain machine-checkable against the README.
follow_ups: Use the enum value the skill asked for to finish help_context, then continue.
